# Work Packages: OPTIONS and Link Header Discovery

**Inputs**: Design documents from `/kitty-specs/019-options-link-discovery/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md
**GitHub Issue**: #102

**Tests**: Included -- the spec requires test coverage via acceptance scenarios.

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`. Treat this file as the high-level checklist; keep deep implementation detail inside the prompt files.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Include precise file paths or modules.

## Path Conventions
- **Source**: `src/`
- **Tests**: `test/`

---

## Work Package WP01: Core Type and Project Scaffolding (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Add `DiscoveryMediaType` struct to Frank core and scaffold the new `Frank.Discovery` project with its `.fsproj`, namespace, and solution integration. This provides the foundation all subsequent WPs depend on.
**Independent Test**: `dotnet build` succeeds for both `Frank` and `Frank.Discovery` projects. The `DiscoveryMediaType` struct is usable from `Frank.Discovery`.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP01-core-type-and-project-scaffolding.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 Add `DiscoveryMediaType` struct record to `src/Frank/Builder.fs`
- [x] T002 Create `src/Frank.Discovery/Frank.Discovery.fsproj` (multi-target net8.0;net9.0;net10.0, project ref to Frank)
- [x] T003 Create `test/Frank.Discovery.Tests/Frank.Discovery.Tests.fsproj` (net10.0, project ref to Frank.Discovery, Expecto + TestHost)
- [x] T004 Create `test/Frank.Discovery.Tests/Program.fs` (Expecto entry point)
- [x] T005 Add Frank.Discovery and Frank.Discovery.Tests projects to `Frank.sln`

### Implementation Notes
- `DiscoveryMediaType` is a `[<Struct>]` record with `MediaType: string` and `Rel: string` fields, placed alongside `ResourceSpec` in `Builder.fs`.
- The `.fsproj` for Frank.Discovery follows the pattern from Frank.Auth: multi-target, `FrameworkReference` to `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App`, project reference to Frank.
- The test `.fsproj` follows Frank.Auth.Tests pattern: net10.0 only, Expecto + TestSdk + TestHost.
- Solution references follow existing solution folder structure (src/ and test/ solution folders).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001 (core type) and T002-T005 (project scaffolding) could be done in parallel once the type signature is agreed upon, but they are small enough to be sequential.

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Version pinning: Use same package versions as Frank.Auth.Tests (Expecto 10.2.3, TestSdk 17.14.1, TestHost 10.0.0).

---

## Work Package WP02: OPTIONS Discovery Middleware (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement `OptionsDiscoveryMiddleware` that generates implicit OPTIONS responses with correct `Allow` header and media type aggregation from endpoint metadata. Implement the `useOptionsDiscovery` custom operation on `WebHostBuilder`.
**Independent Test**: Register a test host with resources having various HTTP methods and `DiscoveryMediaType` metadata. Send OPTIONS requests and verify correct `Allow` headers and pass-through behavior (CORS preflight, explicit handler, no route match).
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP02-options-discovery-middleware.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T006 Create `src/Frank.Discovery/OptionsDiscoveryMiddleware.fs` with full middleware implementation
- [ ] T007 Create `src/Frank.Discovery/WebHostBuilderExtensions.fs` with `useOptionsDiscovery` custom operation
- [ ] T008 Create `test/Frank.Discovery.Tests/OptionsDiscoveryTests.fs` with acceptance tests for US1

### Implementation Notes
- Middleware injects `EndpointDataSource` via DI and `ILogger`.
- On OPTIONS request: check for CORS preflight (`Access-Control-Request-Method` header), explicit handler (`HttpMethodMetadata` containing "OPTIONS"), and no route match (`ctx.GetEndpoint()` null). Pass through in all those cases.
- For implicit OPTIONS: enumerate sibling endpoints by matching `RoutePattern.RawText`, collect HTTP methods from `HttpMethodMetadata`, collect and deduplicate `DiscoveryMediaType` entries, set `Allow` header, return 200 with empty body.
- `useOptionsDiscovery` registers middleware on `spec.Middleware` (runs after routing).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T008 (tests) can start once T006 and T007 are scaffolded.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (needs `DiscoveryMediaType` type and project structure).

### Risks & Mitigations
- `EndpointDataSource` DI availability: In older .NET versions, `EndpointDataSource` may not be registered by default. The middleware should inject `IEnumerable<EndpointDataSource>` or use `CompositeEndpointDataSource` as a fallback. Research R-01 discusses this.
- CORS coexistence: Middleware must check `Access-Control-Request-Method` header per research R-03.

---

## Work Package WP03: Link Header Middleware (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Implement `LinkHeaderMiddleware` that appends RFC 8288 Link headers to 2xx GET/HEAD responses from endpoints with `DiscoveryMediaType` metadata. Add `useLinkHeaders` and `useDiscovery` custom operations.
**Independent Test**: Register a test host with resources having `DiscoveryMediaType` metadata. Send GET/HEAD requests and verify Link headers on 2xx responses. Verify no Link headers on error responses, non-GET/HEAD methods, or unmarked resources.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP03-link-header-middleware.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T009 Create `src/Frank.Discovery/LinkHeaderMiddleware.fs` with full middleware implementation
- [ ] T010 Add `useLinkHeaders` and `useDiscovery` custom operations to `WebHostBuilderExtensions.fs`
- [ ] T011 Create `test/Frank.Discovery.Tests/LinkHeaderTests.fs` with acceptance tests for US2-US4

### Implementation Notes
- Middleware checks endpoint metadata for `DiscoveryMediaType` entries before calling `next`. If none present, calls `next` immediately (zero overhead for unmarked resources -- SC-007).
- After handler execution, checks response status (2xx) and method (GET/HEAD). Appends Link headers in RFC 8288 format: `<{path}>; rel="{Rel}"; type="{MediaType}"`.
- Deduplicates by `(MediaType, Rel)` tuple.
- `useDiscovery` composes `useOptionsDiscovery` and `useLinkHeaders` for convenience.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T009 (Link middleware) can proceed in parallel with WP02 (OPTIONS middleware) since they are independent source files. However, T010 extends the same `WebHostBuilderExtensions.fs` created in WP02.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (needs `WebHostBuilderExtensions.fs` to extend with additional operations).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Link header format: Must follow RFC 8288 exactly. Per research R-02, use separate `Link` headers per media type (not comma-separated).
- Status code check timing: Link headers must be appended _after_ the handler runs but _before_ the response is sent. This is straightforward since `ctx.Response.Headers` can be modified until the response body is flushed.

---

## Work Package WP04: Extension Integration and Edge Cases (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Integrate discovery metadata into Frank.LinkedData and Frank.Statecharts extensions. Add edge case tests covering CORS coexistence, explicit OPTIONS handler precedence, media type deduplication, and HEAD request behavior.
**Independent Test**: Build a test host with LinkedData-marked resources and verify end-to-end OPTIONS and Link header behavior. Verify CORS preflight pass-through. Verify explicit OPTIONS handlers take precedence.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP04-extension-integration-and-edge-cases.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T012 [P] Modify `src/Frank.LinkedData/ResourceBuilderExtensions.fs` to add `DiscoveryMediaType` entries in `linkedData` operation
- [ ] T013 [P] Modify `src/Frank.Statecharts/ResourceBuilderExtensions.fs` to add `DiscoveryMediaType` entries in `stateMachine` operation
- [ ] T014 Create `test/Frank.Discovery.Tests/EdgeCaseTests.fs` with tests for edge cases (CORS, explicit handler, dedup, HEAD, error responses)
- [ ] T015 Update `.fsproj` compile order for test project to include all test files

### Implementation Notes
- Frank.LinkedData adds three `DiscoveryMediaType` entries: `application/ld+json`, `text/turtle`, `application/rdf+xml` (all with `rel="describedby"`).
- Frank.Statecharts adds `DiscoveryMediaType` entry for `application/scxml+xml` (with `rel="describedby"`). Confirm exact media types by inspecting `StateMachineMetadata`.
- Edge case tests cover: CORS preflight detection, explicit handler precedence, empty handler list, multiple Link headers, deduplication, Link on HEAD but not on error responses.
- T012 and T013 are independent of each other and can be done in parallel.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T012 (LinkedData integration) and T013 (Statecharts integration) are fully parallelizable (different files, different packages).
- T014 (edge case tests) depends on WP02 and WP03 middleware being in place.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP03 (needs both middlewares and all WebHostBuilder extensions in place).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Frank.LinkedData and Frank.Statecharts may not compile with a `DiscoveryMediaType` reference if their `.fsproj` files don't reference Frank core properly -- but they already do (both have `ProjectReference` to `../Frank/Frank.fsproj`).
- Statecharts media types: The exact set may differ from the data-model.md candidates. Implementation should inspect the existing code to determine the correct types.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 → WP02 → WP03 → WP04
- **Parallelization**: WP02 and WP03 could theoretically run in parallel (independent middleware files), but WP03's `WebHostBuilderExtensions.fs` extends the file created in WP02, so sequential is safer. Within WP04, T012 and T013 are fully parallel.
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 deliver OPTIONS discovery (US1) -- the foundational capability. WP03 adds Link headers (US2-US4). WP04 wires up the extension packages and hardens edge cases.
